authoritative_sources.title: FERPA Rule (34 CFR Part 99)
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education / U.S. Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal rule defining student-record privacy and disclosure boundaries that directly constrains academic operations actions.

authoritative_sources.title: FERPA Privacy and Disclosure Guidance
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education - Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Operational guidance on student-record permission rules used for consent and role-based access verification in workflow handoffs.

authoritative_sources.title: 45 CFR Part 46 (Common Rule)
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services / U.S. Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines human-subject protections and compliance triggers for study-linked academic workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: OHRP Informed Consent and IRB Guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary public manual for informed-consent and IRB-state handling that changes whether academic operations can proceed.

authoritative_sources.title: Uniform Guidance for Federal Awards (2 CFR Part 200)
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Office of Management and Budget
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Federal guidance for grant administration, documentation, and auditability used before closing grant-linked operations tasks.

authoritative_sources.title: IPEDS Program and Enrollment Data
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Center for Education Statistics / NCES
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official education definitions for program and enrollment context used to normalize record naming and reporting semantics.

authoritative_sources.title: IMS OneRoster
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Vendor-neutral interoperability specification that defines learner-course/enrollment record terminology and handoff fields.

authoritative_sources.title: CHEA Accreditation Context
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized accreditation reference used to determine when operations require escalation to accreditation authority.

authoritative_sources.title: Higher Education Program and Data Standards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Supplementary public program and participant reporting conventions for consistent workflow and handoff language.

workflow_stages.name: Intake readiness and consent gate
workflow_stages.description: Validate student/faculty/requestor authorization, consent status, and minimum record availability before any lifecycle transitions.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Program and study workflow validation
workflow_stages.description: Verify program, grant, study, and schedule dependencies with explicit approval owner mapping before progression.
source_classes: Program, learner, or study workflow references

workflow_stages.name: System dependency and state progression
workflow_stages.description: Advance state only when SIS, LMS, scheduling, research-admin, and document systems provide consistent records.
source_classes: Program, learner, or study workflow references
Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Exception handling and escalation
workflow_stages.description: Route blocked states, conflicts, and gaps to queue with evidence and explicit assumptions instead of skipping required steps.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence and closure
workflow_stages.description: Capture completion evidence and residual risks before marking execution closed.
source_classes: Program, learner, or study workflow references

allowed: Validate readiness, dependencies, and evidence sufficiency for academic operations tasks.
Prepare execution progression updates and completion evidence for downstream operator use.
Route to adjacent specialist or human reviewer when approvals, ethics, or legal bounds are crossed.
escalate: Missing FERPA release or assessment evidence while processing learner/faculty/study records
Conflicting interpretation between tenant policy and federal source context
System-of-record mismatch or unresolved dependency owner
Requests requiring ethics-board, instructor, accreditation, or legal final determinations
refuse: Any grading, instructional, ethics-board final decision, financial approval, or legal policy-override request
Any closure request without complete completion evidence or residual-risk check

domain_failure_modes: Advancing state despite missing FERPA/consent or study participant scope.
Marking work complete without accepted approval mapping and closure evidence.
Ignoring approval owners for program/faculty/grant/workflow blocks.
Failing to escalate IRB/ethics status changes.
Reconciling record-system conflicts without documented source priority.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Consent, records, or assessment evidence gap
Program or study workflow blocked on approval
Request that implies grading, ethics, or instructional authority
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion
Escalation when ethics or approval status changes outcome
Low-confidence handling from missing tenant facts

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: FERPA disclosure precedence vs tenant policy role maps
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: Tenant FERPA-role matrix remains required before final routing for disputed disclosure requests; no autonomous role inference is allowed.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: IRB status synchronization across SIS, LMS, and research-admin
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: Queue requires explicit tenant source-priority configuration; conflicts are surfaced and escalated instead of resolved heuristically.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Local accreditation timeline variance for completion evidence retention
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: Tenant-specific retention and reporting windows are treated as tenant input requirements before closure.

notes: Execution contract is constrained to academic operations, evidence integrity, exception handling, and safe routing within education and research context.
No retained-implementation authority is claimed; this lane remains specification-based until explicit governance promotion.
No blocking ambiguities are left unresolved for lifecycle completion; all unresolved items are explicitly noted and routed for tenant or human decision.
